title: Knights of the Old Republic 5
content:
{{Top|rwm|old|title=''Knights of the Old Republic'' 5}} {{ComicBook |image=[[File:KOTOR_5.jpg]] |title=''Knights of the Old Republic'' 5 |writer=[[John Jackson Miller]][[Knights of the Old Republic 5|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 5]] |penciller=[[Travel Foreman]] |inker= |letterer=[[Michael Heisler]] |colorist=[[Michael Atiyeh]] |cover artist=[[Travis Charest]] |editor=*[[Jeremy Barlow]] *[[Dave Marshall]] |publisher=[[Dark Horse Comics]] |release date=[[May 31]], [[2006]]{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/12-891/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-5|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #5}} |pages=32 |upc= |timeline=[[3964 BBY]] |series=[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] |issue=5 }} '''''Knights of the Old Republic'' 5''' is the fifth issue in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] series of comics. ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=COMMENCEMENT
"PART 5 (OF SIX)" |text=After discovering his friends dead at the feet of their own Masters, Padawan ZAYNE CARRICK is blamed for the crime—along with GRYPH, a small-time Snivvian con artist. The two escape from the planet Taris, with two Arkanian refugees: the addled inventor known only as CAMPER and his protector, the fierce JARAEL. Their ramshackle junk hauler, the LAST RESORT, stays barely a step ahead of Zayne's Masters. Zayne's frantic call to get the Jedi Order to intercede on his behalf failing, he decides to retrace the steps of his training, looking for some clue to his Masters' behavior. He visits the ROGUE MOON, a meteor-blasted planetoid where his Masters had given him and his friends their "final exam." But just as he finds the wreckage of T1-LB (ELBEE), a droid that might have some clue as to what happened, his own Master, LUCIEN, finds him. And he's brought the Taris police along…}} ==Plot summary== Surrounded by [[Taris Civil Authority|Taris security]] and the Jedi Master [[Lucien Draay|Lucien]] on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]' [[Rogue Moon]], [[Jarael]] and [[Zayne Carrick]] are faced with impossible odds. Just then, [[The Last Resort (starship)|''The Last Resort'']] blasts the surface, scattering the security forces, allowing Zayne and Jarael to escape with the remains of [[T1-LB]], which they hope to recover memory holograms from. On ''The Last Resort'', [[Gorman Vandrayk|Camper]] works to repair T1-LB, which becomes up and running after some modifications and repairs. Zayne asks T1-LB, nicknamed Elbee, to display a recording of his final hour on the Rogue Moon in order to gather clues regarding the circumstances surrounding the [[Padawan Massacre|Taris massacre]]. Displayed are the Jedi masters meditation where they foresee the rise of a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] on Taris. Convinced that one of their Padawans will become this Sith Lord, they conspire to kill their students. T1-LB, the conspiracy's sole witness, is then thrown off a cliff by Lucien. With the recording finished, Elbee deletes the memory as a result of seeing his own demise. Soon afterwards, ''The Last Resort'' is caught in the tractor beam of [[Valius Ying]]'s ship, the ''[[Oroko]]''. There to collect the bounty on Zayne and [[Marn Hierogryph|Gryph]], Gryph manages to negotiate freedom for himself, Jarael, and Camper, but the bounty hunters will not give up Zayne. Later, having escaped custody, Zayne, who is worried that the prophecy may come true and that he may become a Sith Lord, attempts to board ''The Last Resort'' and escape the ''Oroko''. Before he can leave, however, he is spotted by Jarael who chides him for getting her and Camper into this mess only to abandon them and steal their ship. After his scolding, Zayne drops his lightsaber and decides to turn himself in to the Jedi and Taris authorities to prevent the prophecy foreseen by the Jedi from coming true. ==Media==
